text.title: Spring Async Examples
text.summary: Worked example converting a synchronous email-send call into a Spring @Async fan-out.

example.context: Brownfield Boot 3.4 monolith; OrderController.placeOrder() inline-calls emailService.send(); p99 latency 800ms; email vendor adds ~400ms.
example.walk: Step 1: confirm send is fire-and-forget (no caller wait) and not @Transactional → eligible; bean split required (OrderController calls notificationService — already separate bean). Step 2: create AsyncConfig with emailExecutor (core=4, max=8, queue=100, CallerRunsPolicy, shutdown drain 60s, TaskDecorator copying MDC + SecurityContext). Step 3: annotate NotificationService.sendOrderConfirmation @Async("emailExecutor") returning CompletableFuture<Void>. Step 4: register executor.* Micrometer metrics + AsyncUncaughtExceptionHandler logging + incrementing notification.error counter. Step 5: test with Awaitility.until() against WireMock SMTP stub; run spring-async-audit.sh → 0 violations.
example.output: p99 latency drops from 800ms to 380ms; notification.error metric visible in Grafana; deploy drains queue gracefully.
